lines.speaker: Keepa Kettilon
lines.text: Oh, hello stranger. If you're new to town you should speak with our ruler, King Gjuki Sorvott IV. He'll want to meet you - he always wants to have a chat with any strangers in town. They could be spies you know.

lines.speaker: Keepa Kettilon
lines.text: Yes, spies from Neitiznot. They're everywhere. Apparently. That's what the King says anyway.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what do you do here?

lines.speaker: Keepa Kettilon
lines.text: Me? I'm a cook. Fish dishes a speciality. I hate fish. But fish is about all we have. Except for yak. But we're not allowed to eat yak.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why not, is yak meat bad for you?

lines.speaker: Keepa Kettilon
lines.text: Certainly not! Yak meat is both sweet and delicately flavoured. Yak is juicier than buffalo and elk - but never gamey. It goes wonderfully well with a glass of red wine, and it's healthy too!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So why aren't you allowed to eat it?

lines.speaker: Keepa Kettilon
lines.text: King's orders. They breed and eat yaks on Neitiznot, so WE'RE not allowed to. It's unjust I tell you! I had a dream last night about a juicy fillet of yak, served on a bed of deep-fried seaweed and topped with a garnish

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's a little sad. So do you have any cooked food I could buy?

lines.speaker: Keepa Kettilon
lines.text: No. It would only be fish anyway. Why would you want to eat fish?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I wouldn't mind a few shark and swordfish steaks as back-up you know.

lines.speaker: Keepa Kettilon
lines.text: If you insist. Sorry the stock is so low, but I just can't be bothered to cook any more.

lines.speaker: Keepa Kettilon
lines.text: Go away and let me wallow in self loathing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you want any help with that? I can loath you as well.
